step.name: receive_idea
step.action: Receive the raw idea or concept from the user. This may be a brief description, a topic, a title, or a detailed outline. Capture the core intent and any constraints mentioned.
step.output: Raw idea document with initial notes

step.name: ask_clarifying_questions
step.action: Ask the user clarifying questions to fill in production parameters: target duration, format type (short-form, long-form, series episode), tone (educational, entertaining, dramatic), target audience, which characters to include, any brand constraints, and preferred visual style direction.

step.name: load_format_preset
step.action: Load the appropriate format preset from {project-root}/_cookie/_config/format-presets.yaml based on the chosen format type. Extract duration constraints, scene structure templates, pacing guidelines, and platform-specific requirements.

step.name: load_existing_characters
step.action: Load existing character identities from {project-root}/characters/ using INDEX_GUIDED strategy. For each character referenced in the idea, load their identity.json to retrieve prompt tokens, personality traits, voice configuration, and visual attributes.
step.output: Character identity data for all referenced characters

step.name: generate_scene_breakdown
step.action: Break the idea into discrete scenes following the format preset structure. Assign each scene a sequential ID, a descriptive title, an estimated duration in seconds, and a scene type (intro, hook, content, transition, outro). Ensure total scene durations sum to the target duration within 10% tolerance.
step.output: Scene breakdown table with IDs, titles, durations, and types

step.name: write_visual_direction
step.action: For each scene, write detailed visual direction including: setting/environment description, character positioning and actions, camera angle and movement, lighting mood, color palette notes, and any VFX or text overlay requirements. Use character prompt tokens where applicable.
step.output: Visual direction block for every scene

step.name: write_audio_direction
step.action: For each scene, write detailed audio direction including: narration text (verbatim), music mood and reference, sound effects list with timing, voice tone/pacing notes, and any silence or pause requirements. Specify which character voice delivers each narration line.
step.output: Audio direction block for every scene

step.name: calculate_total_duration
step.action: Sum all scene durations. Compare against the target duration from the format preset. If the total deviates by more than 10%, flag which scenes should be shortened or extended. Recalculate narration word counts against typical speech rates (150 wpm conversational, 130 wpm dramatic).
step.output: Duration validation report with any adjustment recommendations

step.name: output_canonical_script
step.action: Assemble the complete canonical script in the standard template format. Include: header (title, episode ID, format, duration, characters), scene list with all visual and audio directions, duration summary, and metadata footer. Ensure all character references use the [character:X] placeholder syntax.

step.name: save_script
step.action: Save the canonical script to episodes/{episode_id}/script/canonical-script.md. Create the directory structure if it does not exist. Also save a draft copy with timestamp for version tracking.
step.output: Saved canonical script file and draft backup

quality-gates.gate: Script follows the canonical format template with all required sections
Every scene has duration, setting, characters, visual direction, and audio direction
Total duration is within 10% of target duration from format preset
All character names are valid and exist in the characters directory
Music and SFX directions are specific enough for asset sourcing
Narration word count is feasible for the allocated scene duration
